source_pr.title: variable: add support for timestamp
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #29415 

Problem Summary:

The value returned by `SELECT @@timestamp` is now compatible with MySQL.
See: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.0/en/server-system-variables.html#sysvar_timestamp

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [x] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [x] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it compared results across multiple executions of `SHOW ... VARIABLES` while the `timestamp` variable could change between queries (system-time dependent). This PR stabilizes it by avoiding a second query for the sorted comparison (sort the same result), and by making the timestamp-related integration test deterministic via fixed `time_zone` and stable `NOW(...)` formatting.
fix_pattern: Avoid re-executing time-dependent SHOW queries when computing sorted expectations; make timestamp-related tests deterministic by fixing time zone and using stable NOW(...) formatting.

evidence: files: executor/myc_test.go, executor/seqtest/seq_executor_serial_test.go, executor/set.go, expression/helper.go, expression/helper_test.go, expression/integration_test.go, sessionctx/variable/sysvar.go, sessionctx/variable/sysvar_test.go, sessionctx/variable/tidb_vars.go
